generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor deployed across physical infrastructure to collect real-time temperature data for building environmental monitoring. Integrated with Eclipse Hono middleware for data normalization and BIM model for semantic consistency."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor measuring relative humidity levels in building environments. Feeds data through Eclipse Hono middleware to maintain consistency with BIM-based digital twin representation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor detecting presence and occupancy patterns within building spaces. Data stream normalized via Eclipse Hono for integration with digital twin visualization and control systems."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance LightingSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor measuring lighting conditions (illuminance) to support adaptive lighting control systems. Embedded sensor configurations within BIM model for automated data extraction."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightingDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Data Transmitted
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance HumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance LightingDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightingSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Building Information Model containing static representations of physical infrastructure including geometry, spatial relationships, and embedded sensor/actuator configurations for semantic consistency."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMToolEnabler
]

instance RuntimeDataModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic model enriched with real-time operational data including sensor readings, control logic, and device metadata extracted from BIM during deployment."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoMiddlewareEnabler
]

instance ControlLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "BIM-defined model containing executable control policies (e.g., lighting schedules, HVAC setpoints) transformed into runtime artifacts for adaptive building systems."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoMiddlewareEnabler
]

instance SimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics model for energy consumption forecasting and scenario testing, visualized through Unreal Engine 5 for decision support."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngineEnabler
]

instance ConfigurationMetadata : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Standardized metadata embedded in BIM defining device integration parameters (tenant IDs, endpoint addresses) for automated deployment with Eclipse Hono/Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:fromData BIMDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DeploymentEnabler
]

instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time temperature readings from IoT sensors processed through Eclipse Hono middleware, used for environmental monitoring and control optimization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoMiddlewareEnabler
]

instance HumidityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time humidity measurements integrated with building control systems for maintaining optimal indoor environmental conditions."
    DTDFVocab:fromData HumidityDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoMiddlewareEnabler
]

instance OccupancyData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Occupancy patterns data enabling energy-efficient building operations by correlating presence with lighting/HVAC control systems."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoMiddlewareEnabler
]

instance LightingData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Lighting condition measurements supporting adaptive lighting control systems optimized for energy efficiency and visual comfort."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LightingDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoMiddlewareEnabler
]

instance BIMDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BIMModel
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHonoMiddlewareEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono middleware platform aggregating and normalizing real-time data streams from heterogeneous IoT sensors for seamless integration with digital twin components."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveMaintenanceService, StakeholderDashboardService
]

instance EclipseDittoMiddlewareEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Ditto service-oriented middleware enabling bidirectional communication between digital twin and physical systems via MQTT, REST, and WebSocket protocols."
    DTDFVocab:enables AdaptiveControlService, AutomatedCommissioningService, ConfigurationManagementService
]

instance UnrealEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unreal Engine 5 platform providing immersive 3D visualization for interactive monitoring and control of building digital twin representations."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, StakeholderDashboardService, SimulationService
]

instance ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MDE tools automating transformations of BIM models into runtime artifacts including device configurations and API endpoints for digital twin deployment."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeploymentService, ConfigurationManagementService
]

instance BIMToolEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "BIM tools (e.g., Revit, ArchiCAD) used for device modeling and control logic definition within building information models."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeviceModelingService, ModelValidationService
]

instance DeploymentEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automated deployment framework synchronizing digital twin components with physical systems using standardized middleware interfaces."
    DTDFVocab:enables RuntimeArtifactGenerationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing immersive 3D visualization of building operations through Unreal Engine 5 for real-time monitoring of environmental conditions and system performance."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnvironmentalConditionInsight, OccupancyPatternInsight
]

instance AdaptiveControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing energy-efficient control policies through reinforcement learning algorithms (e.g., BOREALIS project) for dynamic lighting and HVAC optimization."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyOptimizationAction, VisualComfortAction
]

instance AutomatedCommissioningService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service automating configuration and commissioning of Building Control System devices through model-driven transformations from BIM models."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviceConfigurationAction
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service analyzing sensor data patterns to identify potential equipment failures and recommend maintenance actions before critical failures occur."
    DTDFVocab:provides EquipmentFailureAlertInsight
]

instance StakeholderDashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing visualized dashboards for facility managers to monitor and control building operations through interactive 3D representations."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalAnalyticsInsight, ControlPolicyUpdateAction
]

instance ConfigurationManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling dynamic updates to digital twin components including adding new sensors or modifying control policies without full redeployment."
    DTDFVocab:provides RuntimeConfigurationAction
]

instance DeploymentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service handling automated deployment of digital twin artifacts including device configurations and middleware integrations based on BIM models."
    DTDFVocab:provides RuntimeArtifactGenerationAction
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service executing predictive simulations for energy consumption forecasting and scenario testing using enriched digital twin data models."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyForecastInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance EnvironmentalConditionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time operational analytics of temperature and humidity conditions providing basis for environmental control decisions."
]

instance OccupancyPatternInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of occupancy patterns enabling optimized building operations by correlating presence with energy consumption patterns."
]

instance EquipmentFailureAlertInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance alerts generated from sensor data anomalies indicating potential equipment failures requiring preventive maintenance."
]

instance EnergyOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of lighting and HVAC systems to minimize energy consumption while maintaining occupant comfort levels."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance VisualComfortAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic lighting adjustments based on occupancy and natural light conditions to maintain optimal visual comfort standards."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DeviceConfigurationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated configuration of Building Control System devices through standardized model-driven transformations from BIM specifications."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance OperationalAnalyticsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comprehensive analytics dashboard showing energy consumption trends, occupancy patterns, and environmental conditions for facility management."
]

instance ControlPolicyUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Iterative refinement of control strategies through reinforcement learning models based on runtime feedback from building operations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance RuntimeConfigurationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic updates to digital twin components including sensor additions and control policy modifications without requiring full system redeployment."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance RuntimeArtifactGenerationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated generation of deployable digital twin artifacts (e.g., Ditto device configurations, Hono data streams) from BIM models using model-driven engineering tools."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnergyForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics results forecasting energy consumption patterns based on current operational conditions and historical data trends."
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System actuator controlling lighting levels based on digital twin control policies and real-time sensor feedback for energy optimization."
]

instance HVACController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System controller managing HVAC systems according to adaptive control strategies generated by the digital twin for energy-efficient operation."
]

// C1: Environment
instance BuildingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains TemperatureSensor, HumiditySensor, OccupancySensor, LightingSensor,
                LightingActuator, HVACController, BIMModel, RuntimeDataModel, ControlLogicModel,
                BuildingControlSystem
]

instance BuildingControlSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical infrastructure integrating sensors, actuators, and controllers that execute commands from the digital twin to influence building operations."
    base:isContainedIn BuildingEnvironment
]

// C5: Twinning Process Components
instance DeviceModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling Building Control Engineers to define sensors, actuators, and control logic within BIM models as foundation for digital twin deployment."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviceDefinitionInsight
]

instance ModelValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service validating BIM models for semantic consistency and completeness including presence of required metadata for digital twin integration."
    DTDFVocab:provides ValidationResultInsight
]

instance RuntimeArtifactGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service transforming validated BIM models into deployable digital twin artifacts including Ditto device configurations and Hono data streams."
    DTDFVocab:provides ArtifactGenerationAction
]

instance DeviceDefinitionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Semantic definitions of building devices including sensor specifications, actuator capabilities, and control logic requirements extracted from BIM models."
]

instance ValidationResultInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Results of model validation process indicating semantic consistency and completeness of BIM models for digital twin deployment."
]

instance ArtifactGenerationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated generation of runtime artifacts from BIM models enabling seamless integration with middleware platforms and physical systems."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C7: Multiplicities
instance CentralizedDigitalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Centralized digital twin aggregating data from multiple physical assets for holistic analysis including building-wide energy optimization and comprehensive monitoring."
    base:contains BIMModel, RuntimeDataModel, ControlLogicModel
    base:isContainedIn BuildingEnvironment
]

instance DecentralizedMicroTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Decentralized digital twin for individual building components (e.g., room-level lighting systems) enabling reduced latency and improved scalability."
    base:contains LightingData, LightingActuator
    base:isContainedIn BuildingEnvironment
]

instance HybridDigitalTwinConfiguration : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Hybrid architecture combining centralized twins for building-wide analysis with decentralized micro-twins for component-level control based on operational requirements."
    base:contains CentralizedDigitalTwin, DecentralizedMicroTwin
    base:isContainedIn BuildingEnvironment
]

// C12: System Under Study
instance ControlOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing adaptive control optimization algorithms for energy-efficient building operations based on real-time sensor data and predictive analytics."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyOptimizationAction
]

instance BuildingDigitalTwinSystem : DTDFVocab:SystemUnderStudy [
    base:desc "Building Information Model-based Digital Twin system for real-time monitoring, simulation, and lifecycle optimization of physical infrastructure in AECO domain."
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment BuildingEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Virtual-to-physical interaction occurs via **runtime-generated control signals** sent from the DT to physical actuators. The framework uses **model-driven deployment** to generate runtime interfaces (e.g., RESTful APIs or MQTT topics) that translate virtual commands (e.g., lighting adjustments, HVAC setpoints) into executable actions for the BCS. This interaction is enabled by **Eclipse Ditto**, which provides a standardized middleware for bidirectional communication, ensuring low-latency and reliable control. The framework also supports **custom service integration**, allowing DT engineers to extend functionality (e.g., reinforcement learning-based control policies) without modifying core infrastructure."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DT operates on **real-time and near-real-time scales**, with synchronization frequencies determined by the criticality of the physical system. For example:
   - **High-frequency interactions** (e.g., lighting adjustments) occur at **sub-second intervals** (e.g., 100ms–1s) to support adaptive control.
   - **Medium-frequency interactions** (e.g., environmental monitoring) occur at **second-to-minute intervals** (e.g., 1–60s).
   - **Low-frequency interactions** (e.g., energy consumption analytics) occur at **hourly or daily intervals** for long-term optimization. The framework supports **configurable time scales** via middleware (Eclipse Hono/Ditto) to balance responsiveness and resource usage."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is orchestrated as a **service-oriented, modular architecture** with the following components:
   - **BIM Backbone**: The semantic foundation for device modeling and control logic.
   - **Middleware Layer (Eclipse Hono/Ditto)**: Handles **real-time data exchange, device management, and bidirectional communication**.
   - **Runtime Interface Generator**: Automates the creation of **APIs and protocols** (e.g., MQTT, REST) for physical-virtual interaction.
   - **Visualization Layer (Unreal Engine 5)**: Provides **immersive 3D interfaces** for stakeholders (e.g., facility managers, engineers).
   - **Extension Points**: Standardized interfaces for integrating **custom services** (e.g., AI-based analytics, third-party IoT platforms). The constellation supports **scalable deployment** across entire infrastructure systems (e.g., campus-wide buildings) by leveraging BIM’s hierarchical modeling capabilities."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT spans **multiple lifecycle stages** of the physical asset, from **design to operation and refurbishment**:
   1. **Design phase**: BIM models are enriched with **device and control logic metadata** for automated DT deployment.
   2. **Construction phase**: Physical sensors and actuators are integrated, and the DT is validated using the **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric**.
   3. **Operation phase**: The DT enables **real-time monitoring, predictive analytics, and adaptive control** (e.g., energy optimization via reinforcement learning).
   4. **Refurbishment phase**: The DT supports **dynamic updates** (e.g., adding new sensors or modifying control policies) without disrupting ongoing operations. The framework ensures **semantic continuity** across stages by leveraging BIM as the foundational model."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
   - **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric**: Quantifies the alignment between the physical and virtual representations by comparing sensor data and control outputs.
   - **Model validation**: BIM models are checked for **semantic completeness** (e.g., presence of device metadata) before deployment.
   - **Runtime synchronization**: Eclipse Hono/Ditto enforce **real-time data consistency** between physical and virtual systems.
   - **Uncertainty handling**: The framework supports **probabilistic modeling** (e.g., for sensor noise) and **adaptive control policies** (e.g., reinforcement learning) to mitigate errors. Validation is performed during the **Living Lab case study** to assess the DT’s accuracy and responsiveness."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT employs **MQTT-based protocols** (via Eclipse Hono) for **low-latency, bidirectional communication** between physical sensors/actuators and the virtual model. The middleware supports:
   - **MQTT v5.0** for reliable message delivery and QoS levels.
   - **RESTful APIs** for configuration management and device registration.
   - **WebSocket connections** for interactive visualization (e.g., Unreal Engine 5). The framework uses **standardized device metadata** (e.g., tenant IDs, endpoint addresses) extracted from BIM to establish secure, authenticated connections. Network architectures are designed for **scalability**, supporting **millions of devices** across large infrastructure systems."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is hosted on a **cloud-native infrastructure** with the following deployment characteristics:
   - **Middleware Layer (Eclipse Hono/Ditto)**: Deployed as **containerized microservices** (e.g., Docker/Kubernetes) for scalability and fault tolerance.
   - **Visualization Layer (Unreal Engine 5)**: Runs on **high-performance GPUs** for real-time rendering and interaction.
   - **BIM Integration**: Hosted on **enterprise-grade servers** (e.g., AWS, Azure) to support large-scale BIM models.
   - **Runtime Artifacts**: Generated on-demand via **MDE transformations** and deployed as **serverless functions** or **edge devices** (e.g., for low-latency control). The framework supports **hybrid cloud/edge deployment** to balance latency and resource constraints."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The DT supports **horizontal integration** with external systems via:
   - **Standardized APIs (REST/MQTT)**: Enable interoperability with **third-party IoT platforms, ERP systems, or building management software**.
   - **Ontology-based semantics**: BIM models are enriched with **domain-specific metadata** (e.g., device types, control logic) to facilitate semantic alignment with external systems.
   - **Extension Points**: Allow integration of **custom services** (e.g., AI models, analytics tools) without modifying core infrastructure. The framework adheres to **AECO-specific standards** (e.g., IFC, COBie) to ensure compatibility with legacy systems."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership and privacy are addressed through:
   - **BIM-centric governance**: The **physical asset owner** retains control over BIM models and operational data, while **DT service providers** manage runtime artifacts (e.g., sensor streams, control logic).
   - **Role-based access control (RBAC)**: Implemented via Eclipse Ditto to restrict data access to authorized stakeholders (e.g., facility managers, engineers).
   - **Data anonymization**: Sensors and models support **privacy-preserving techniques** (e.g., differential privacy) for sensitive data (e.g., occupancy patterns).
   - **Compliance frameworks**: Aligns with **GDPR, ISO/IEC 27001**, and **AECO-specific regulations** (e.g., building energy codes). The framework does not specify ownership of **processed insights** (e.g., analytics reports), which may require contractual agreements between stakeholders."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT adheres to the following standards:
   - **Building Information Modeling (BIM)**: Uses **IFC (Industry Foundation Classes)** and **COBie (Construction Operations Building Information Exchange)** for semantic consistency.
   - **IoT and Middleware**: Complies with **OMA LWM2M** (Lightweight M2M) for device management and **MQTT v5.0** for messaging.
   - **Service-Oriented Architectures**: Follows **SOAP/RESTful principles** for API design and **OASIS Open Service Bus (OSB)** for interoperability.
   - **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE)**: Leverages **MOF (Meta-Object Facility)** and **QVT (Queries, Views, Transformations)** for automated model transformations.
   - **AECO-Specific Protocols**: Integrates with **BACnet** and **KNX** for Building Control System (BCS) interoperability. The framework provides **standardized extension points** to accommodate emerging standards (e.g., **ISO 23247 for DTs**)."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are addressed through:
   - **Authentication and Authorization**: Eclipse Ditto implements **JWT-based authentication** and **role-based access control (RBAC)** to secure DT interactions.
   - **Data Encryption**: All communications use **TLS 1.3** for end-to-end encryption, and sensitive data (e.g., sensor streams) is encrypted at rest.
   - **Isolation Mechanisms**: The middleware (Eclipse Hono/Ditto) supports **tenant isolation** to prevent cross-contamination between DTs of different assets.
   - **Safety Protocols**: The framework includes **fail-safe mechanisms** (e.g., fallback to default control policies) to prevent unsafe operations (e.g., unintended actuator activation). **Remote operation risks** are mitigated by requiring **multi-factor authentication (MFA)** for critical commands. The DT does not support **unattended remote execution** of physical operations without human oversight."
]
